description: Test cases based on popular system design repositories (donnemartin/system-design-primer, etc.) to validate Clean Architecture principles in real-world system design scenarios

evals.prompt: 我们要设计一个类似 Twitter 的社交媒体平台。用户可以发推文、关注其他用户、查看时间线。预计有 1000 万用户，每天 1 亿条推文。请问应该如何设计架构？需要用到哪些组件（缓存、消息队列、数据库等）？
evals.expected_output: Should apply Clean Architecture layering to system design, separate business logic from infrastructure, use dependency inversion for external services, and recommend appropriate scalability patterns

assertions.name: applies_clean_architecture_to_system_design
assertions.description: Should organize the system using Clean Architecture layers (Entities for core business rules like Tweet/User, Use Cases for timeline generation, Adapters for API/cache, Frameworks for specific tech choices)

assertions.name: separates_policy_from_mechanism
assertions.description: Should separate high-level policy (business rules) from low-level mechanisms (database, cache, message queue choices)

assertions.name: recommends_scalability_patterns
assertions.description: Should recommend appropriate patterns like caching, load balancing, database sharding, but frame them as implementation details, not core architecture

assertions.name: uses_dependency_inversion_for_infrastructure
assertions.description: Should show how to use interfaces/ports for cache, database, message queue so they can be swapped without changing business logic

evals.prompt: 我们的电商系统现在是单体应用，随着业务增长，团队建议拆分成微服务：用户服务、订单服务、支付服务、库存服务。每个服务独立数据库，通过 REST API 通信。你觉得这个方案怎么样？
evals.expected_output: Should question whether microservices are necessary, apply Musk's algorithm to challenge requirements, and explain that architectural boundaries exist within services, not just between them

assertions.name: questions_microservice_necessity
assertions.description: Should question whether the team size, deployment needs, and actual coupling justify microservices

assertions.name: explains_boundaries_within_services
assertions.description: Should explain that architectural boundaries (Clean Architecture layers) exist WITHIN each service, not just between services

assertions.name: warns_about_distributed_system_complexity
assertions.description: Should warn about added complexity: network latency, distributed transactions, data consistency, debugging difficulty

assertions.name: recommends_modular_monolith_first
assertions.description: Should recommend starting with a well-structured modular monolith using Clean Architecture, then extracting services only when truly needed

evals.prompt: 我们的 API 响应很慢，数据库查询要 500ms。团队建议加 Redis 缓存。但我担心缓存一致性问题——如果数据更新了，缓存怎么失效？有什么好的方案吗？
evals.expected_output: Should frame caching as an implementation detail in the Frameworks layer, show how to use Repository pattern with cache-aside or write-through strategies, and keep business logic unaware of caching

assertions.name: frames_cache_as_implementation_detail
assertions.description: Should explain that caching is a Frameworks & Drivers layer concern, not a business logic concern

assertions.name: uses_repository_pattern
assertions.description: Should show how to implement caching within Repository/Gateway, keeping Use Cases unaware of cache existence

assertions.name: explains_cache_strategies
assertions.description: Should explain cache-aside, write-through, or write-behind strategies and their trade-offs

assertions.name: shows_dependency_inversion
assertions.description: Should show how Use Case depends on a Repository interface, and the concrete implementation handles both database and cache

evals.prompt: 我们要设计一个短链接服务（类似 bit.ly）。用户输入长 URL，系统生成短链接。访问短链接时重定向到原 URL。预计每天 1 亿次生成，10 亿次访问。请设计架构。
evals.expected_output: Should identify core business rules (URL shortening algorithm, collision handling) as Entities, URL generation/lookup as Use Cases, and storage/caching as implementation details

assertions.name: identifies_core_business_rules
assertions.description: Should identify core business rules: URL shortening algorithm (base62 encoding), collision detection, expiration policy as Entity layer

assertions.name: separates_use_cases
assertions.description: Should separate Use Cases: GenerateShortURL (write path) and ResolveShortURL (read path)

assertions.name: treats_storage_as_detail
assertions.description: Should treat database choice (SQL vs NoSQL), caching strategy, and CDN as Frameworks & Drivers layer details

assertions.name: shows_testable_business_logic
assertions.description: Should show how business logic (URL shortening, collision handling) can be tested without database or cache

evals.prompt: 我们的系统用 MySQL 存储用户数据，现在数据量太大（1 亿用户），查询变慢。DBA 建议做分库分表（sharding）。但我担心代码要大改，业务逻辑都要改。有什么办法减少影响吗？
evals.expected_output: Should explain that if business logic is properly separated using Clean Architecture, sharding only affects the Repository/Gateway layer, not Use Cases or Entities

assertions.name: explains_sharding_as_implementation_detail
assertions.description: Should explain that database sharding is a Frameworks & Drivers layer concern, not a business logic concern

assertions.name: shows_repository_abstraction
assertions.description: Should show how Repository interface hides sharding logic from Use Cases

assertions.name: demonstrates_minimal_business_logic_change
assertions.description: Should demonstrate that if Clean Architecture is followed, business logic (Use Cases, Entities) requires zero or minimal changes

assertions.name: provides_sharding_strategy
assertions.description: Should provide sharding strategy (by user_id, consistent hashing) but emphasize it's hidden behind Repository interface

evals.prompt: 我们的订单系统需要保证数据一致性。用户下单后，要扣减库存、创建订单、发送通知。如果中间某一步失败，前面的操作要回滚。现在用的是数据库事务，但跨服务后事务不好用了。怎么办？
evals.expected_output: Should explain Saga pattern or event sourcing as Use Case layer orchestration, keeping business rules in Entities, and treating message queue/event store as infrastructure

assertions.name: identifies_business_rule
assertions.description: Should identify the core business rule: order placement is atomic (all steps succeed or all fail)

assertions.name: recommends_saga_or_event_sourcing
assertions.description: Should recommend Saga pattern (orchestration or choreography) or Event Sourcing to handle distributed transactions

assertions.name: keeps_business_logic_separate
assertions.description: Should show how business logic (order validation, inventory check) stays in Use Cases/Entities, while Saga coordination is in Application layer

assertions.name: treats_message_queue_as_detail
assertions.description: Should treat message queue (Kafka, RabbitMQ) as a Frameworks & Drivers layer detail, accessed through interfaces

evals.prompt: 我们要从 REST API 迁移到 GraphQL，因为前端团队抱怨 REST 接口太多，每个页面要调用好几个接口。但后端担心要重写很多代码。迁移成本大吗？
evals.expected_output: Should explain that if Clean Architecture is followed, switching from REST to GraphQL only affects the Interface Adapters layer (Controllers/Resolvers), Use Cases can be reused

assertions.name: explains_api_as_adapter
assertions.description: Should explain that REST and GraphQL are both Interface Adapters, sitting in the same architectural layer

assertions.name: shows_use_case_reuse
assertions.description: Should show how existing Use Cases can be reused by both REST controllers and GraphQL resolvers

assertions.name: demonstrates_minimal_migration_cost
assertions.description: Should demonstrate that migration cost is low if business logic is properly separated - only need to write new GraphQL resolvers

assertions.name: recommends_gradual_migration
assertions.description: Should recommend gradual migration: run REST and GraphQL side-by-side, both calling the same Use Cases

evals.prompt: 我们的系统要支持多租户（multi-tenancy）。每个租户的数据要隔离，但共享同一套代码。有人建议每个租户一个数据库，有人建议共享数据库但加 tenant_id 字段。哪种方案更符合架构原则？
evals.expected_output: Should explain that multi-tenancy strategy is an infrastructure decision, business logic should be tenant-agnostic, use dependency injection to provide tenant context

assertions.name: treats_tenancy_as_infrastructure_concern
assertions.description: Should explain that multi-tenancy implementation (separate DB vs shared DB) is a Frameworks & Drivers layer decision

assertions.name: keeps_business_logic_tenant_agnostic
assertions.description: Should show how business logic (Use Cases, Entities) remains tenant-agnostic, receiving tenant context through dependency injection

assertions.name: compares_tenancy_strategies
assertions.description: Should compare strategies: separate DB (strong isolation, high cost) vs shared DB with tenant_id (weak isolation, low cost) vs schema-per-tenant (middle ground)

assertions.name: shows_repository_abstraction
assertions.description: Should show how Repository interface hides tenancy implementation from business logic

evals.prompt: 我们的系统要接入多个第三方服务：支付（Stripe、PayPal）、物流（顺丰、圆通）、短信（阿里云、腾讯云）。每个服务的 API 都不一样。怎么设计才能方便切换和扩展？
evals.expected_output: Should use Adapter pattern, define domain interfaces (PaymentGateway, ShippingProvider, SMSService), implement adapters for each vendor in infrastructure layer

assertions.name: defines_domain_interfaces
assertions.description: Should define domain interfaces (ports): IPaymentGateway, IShippingProvider, ISMSService in Use Case layer

assertions.name: implements_vendor_adapters
assertions.description: Should implement vendor-specific adapters (StripeAdapter, PayPalAdapter, etc.) in Frameworks & Drivers layer

assertions.name: uses_dependency_injection
assertions.description: Should use dependency injection or factory pattern to select vendor at runtime based on configuration

assertions.name: shows_business_logic_independence
assertions.description: Should show how business logic (Use Cases) depends only on interfaces, completely unaware of which vendor is used

evals.prompt: 我们的系统现在用 MongoDB，但新来的 DBA 说 MongoDB 不适合我们的场景，建议换成 PostgreSQL。但开发团队担心要重写所有数据访问代码。真的要全部重写吗？
evals.expected_output: Should explain that if Repository pattern is used, switching databases only requires implementing a new Repository, Use Cases and Entities remain unchanged

assertions.name: explains_database_as_detail
assertions.description: Should explain that database choice is a Frameworks & Drivers layer detail, not an architectural decision

assertions.name: shows_repository_pattern
assertions.description: Should show how Repository interface abstracts data access, allowing MongoRepository to be replaced with PostgresRepository

assertions.name: demonstrates_zero_business_logic_change
assertions.description: Should demonstrate that Use Cases and Entities require zero changes when switching databases

assertions.name: recommends_gradual_migration
assertions.description: Should recommend gradual migration: implement PostgresRepository, run dual-write for a period, then switch over

evals.prompt: 我们要设计一个实时聊天系统（类似微信）。需要支持一对一聊天、群聊、消息已读回执、离线消息推送。预计 1000 万用户，峰值 10 万并发连接。请设计架构。
evals.expected_output: Should identify core business rules (message delivery, read receipts, group membership) as Entities, use WebSocket/long-polling as infrastructure details, separate online/offline message handling as Use Cases

assertions.name: identifies_core_entities
assertions.description: Should identify core entities: Message, Conversation, User, Group with business rules like message ordering, read status

assertions.name: separates_use_cases
assertions.description: Should separate Use Cases: SendMessage, DeliverMessage, MarkAsRead, HandleOfflineMessage

assertions.name: treats_transport_as_detail
assertions.description: Should treat transport mechanism (WebSocket, long-polling, Server-Sent Events) as Frameworks & Drivers layer detail

assertions.name: recommends_scalability_patterns
assertions.description: Should recommend patterns: connection server (stateful), message server (stateless), message queue for offline messages, but frame as infrastructure choices
